transcript:
David: Thanks for coming in. Jennifer and I wanted to explore Careerspan together since this touches both engineering and HR.

V: Makes sense. Tell me about what's driving your interest?

David: We're scaling fast. 200 engineers now, planning to be at 350 by end of next year. I'm worried about retention and growth paths. We're losing mid-level engineers to Google and Meta.

Jennifer: From the HR side, our engagement scores are dropping. Engineers don't feel they're growing fast enough. Exit interviews consistently mention lack of career development.

V: How are you handling career development now?

David: Managers are supposed to do it but honestly, they're overwhelmed. Most came up through IC roles and aren't trained in coaching. They give feedback on code but not on career strategy.

Jennifer: We tried building an internal career development program last year. Created frameworks, levels, competencies. Engineers said it was too generic - didn't feel personalized.

V: That's common. Have you looked at other career coaching solutions?

David: We tried BetterUp two years ago.

V: What happened?

Jennifer: It didn't stick. Coaches weren't technical enough. Engineers complained they were getting life coaching, not engineering career coaching. Usage dropped after 3 months.

V: That's exactly why we built Careerspan differently. All our coaches have 10+ years in tech. Most were engineering leaders themselves. They understand IC vs management tracks, promotion timelines, comp negotiations in tech.

David: That's compelling. What does engagement look like?

V: Each engineer gets a dedicated coach. Monthly 1-hour video sessions. Between sessions, async support via our platform for questions that come up.

Jennifer: What about managers? Do they get visibility?

V: We keep coaching confidential but coaches can facilitate conversations between engineers and managers if the engineer wants. We also offer quarterly manager training on career development conversations.

David: Pricing?

V: $295 per person per month. That includes unlimited engineers.

David: So for 200 engineers that's $59k/month, about $700k annually.

V: Correct. Most companies see 20-30% improvement in retention. If you're losing engineers to Google, what's your replacement cost?

Jennifer: All-in with recruiting, lost productivity, training... probably $150k per engineer.

David: We lost 18 engineers last year. If we prevent even 5 of those...

V: You break even. And you keep your top talent.

Jennifer: What's implementation like?

V: 2-3 weeks to onboard. We assign coaches, engineers book their first session, and we're off. Very lightweight on your team.

David: Jennifer, what do you think?

Jennifer: I like it. Much more targeted than BetterUp was. The tech-specific coaching is the differentiator.

David: Agreed. V, can you send over a proposal? Include the 200 engineers now, but show pricing if we scale to 350.

V: Absolutely. I'll get that over by end of week. Any other questions?

Jennifer: Timeline - when could we start if we decide to move forward?

V: We could start in January. That gives us December to onboard and assign coaches.

David: Perfect. Send the proposal and let's regroup.

V: Will do. Thanks for the time.
